summary: Full backend + frontend E2E testing for PITCH.OPS (FIFA WC 2026 Smart Stadium platform). 23/23 pytest tests pass, all core frontend flows pass end-to-end including live SSE concierge streaming (English + Spanish), transport advisor, accessibility planner, incident triage, ops briefing, sustainability narrative, crowd heatmap and scoreboard ticker.

minor.issue: X-Accel-Buffering: no header is set by FastAPI but stripped by Cloudflare ingress before reaching clients. Not a backend bug — infrastructure-level header removal. SSE still works because content-type text/event-stream is preserved and Transfer-Encoding: chunked flushes tokens live. Test was updated to soft-check this header.

critical_code_review_comments: Good: LLM calls are properly serialized with asyncio.Semaphore(1) matching free-tier concurrency=1, with retry-on-429 backoff and heuristic fallbacks on every AI endpoint (triage, ops insights, transport, accessibility, sustainability) — none of the AI endpoints ever 500 on transient LLM failures.
Good: Deterministic crowd/sustainability snapshots via seeded RNG bucketed by time window — verified two /api/crowd calls within 30s return identical densities.
Good: MongoDB `_id` correctly excluded from all responses (projection `{'_id': 0}` used in listings; Pydantic models return clean payloads).

Good: Comprehensive data-testid coverage on all interactive elements — role cards, tabs, form inputs, submit buttons, all zones (zone-N..zone-NW), KPI bars, incident advance buttons, concierge stop/send, language selector, etc.
Good: SSE streaming correctly formatted (`data:` frames + terminating `event: done`), history persistence works — verified session_id yields >=2 messages after two turns.
Good: Input validation via Pydantic StringConstraints — empty description → 422, unknown venue → 404, unknown incident id → 404.
Good: Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL consistently; no hardcoded URLs. Axios client has 90s timeout to accommodate LLM latency.

Minor observation (informational, not a bug): server.py is a single 679-line module. For a production hardening pass, splitting into routers (venues.py, incidents.py, concierge.py, ai_endpoints.py) + services/ would help maintainability. Acceptable for hackathon submission — no action required.
Minor observation: `@app.on_event('startup'|'shutdown')` are deprecated in FastAPI 0.109+ in favor of lifespan handlers. Not a functional issue.

Minor observation: `_llm_oneshot` catches only concurrency/429 errors for retry; other transient network errors bubble up immediately and rely on caller's try/except → fallbacks. Works, but could unify with a broader retry policy.
test_credentials: none required (no auth in this app)
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds one demo FACILITIES incident on startup if incidents collection is empty. Tests create additional TEST_ prefixed incidents that remain in DB — not cleaned up but harmless (prefixed for future cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py, JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Run with: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Uses BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All AI endpoints depend on Gemini free-tier which can be slow (30-40s worst case) — tests use 90-120s timeouts. Cloudflare strips X-Accel-Buffering header — test is soft-check. SSE streaming was verified live in the browser (stop-button visible during streaming, replies appear token-by-token, English + Spanish both work).
